intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll process the first sample (GSM5746259, Patient 145 Day 0) with scanpy. Let me start by reading the relevant recipe and then presenting a plan.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Now let me present the plan:

input.title: Standard scanpy QC + Clustering for GSM5746259
input.summary: Single-sample scRNA-seq preprocessing, QC filtering, normalization, HVG selection, dimensionality reduction, and Leiden clustering.
input.rationale: Produce QC plots, clustering, and marker genes to understand the cell-type composition and data quality of the first COVID-19 patient sample.
input.assumptions:
- Processing GSM5746259 (Patient 145, Day 0) in isolation
- Mitochondrial genes identified by 'MT-' prefix (standard for human genes)
- QC thresholds: n_genes ≥ 200, mtDNA% < 20% (standard for PBMCs)
- Leiden clustering with default resolution (1.0)
- Output figures in PNG format (150 DPI)

steps.title: Load count matrix for first sample
steps.description: Load GSM5746259 10x MTX from the registered dataset directory
expected_outputs: AnnData object with raw counts, ~7500 cells × 36k genes

steps.title: Calculate QC metrics
steps.description: Compute n_genes, n_counts, pct_counts_mt using sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics
expected_outputs: QC metrics in adata.obs and adata.var

steps.title: QC filtering
steps.description: Filter cells: n_genes ≥ 200, pct_counts_mt < 20%; filter genes: n_cells ≥ 3

steps.title: Highly variable genes (HVG)
expected_outputs: HVG info in adata.var, HVG plot

steps.title: Marker gene detection
steps.description: sc.tl.rank_genes_groups to find top genes per cluster

steps.title: Generate plots
steps.description: HVG plot, PCA variance, UMAP by cluster, cluster sizes
